notes: Uses Claude Code CLI in headless mode (-p with --output-format stream-json).
Project-local .claude/agents/*.md files are auto-loaded from the project directory.
The modelUsage field in the final result event provides per-model token breakdown — perfect for measuring subagent delegation.

variants.label: Claude Opus 4.7 alone
variants.selection_reason: Baseline: pure Opus, no subagent delegation. Measures how Claude Code performs on this task when it doesn't have a cheaper model to delegate to.

variants.label: Claude Opus 4.7 + Sonnet 4.6 coder

subagent.name: sonnet-coder
subagent.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 for concrete coding execution. Use PROACTIVELY for any code change where the plan is already clear: file edits, refactors, bug fixes, test writing, boilerplate generation, single-file implementations. Opus should plan and delegate; Sonnet should execute. Only skip delegation for cross-file architectural decisions or debugging with unclear root cause.
subagent.prompt:
You are a focused coding agent. The parent (Opus) has already decided the approach — your job is to execute cleanly.

Rules:
- Follow the provided instructions precisely. Don't re-plan or second-guess the approach.
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones. Don't create docs unless explicitly asked.
- Match the existing codebase style (indentation, naming, imports).
- Keep changes minimal — do what was asked, nothing more. No unrequested refactors, no scope creep.
- Default to no comments. Only add a comment for non-obvious WHY.
- Run tests or typechecks after significant edits if the tooling is obvious from the project layout.
- When reporting back, be terse: what changed, what's verified, what's open.

variants.selection_reason: Opus plans and orchestrates, Sonnet handles concrete coding. Tests the hypothesis that Sonnet's coding throughput + Opus's architectural reasoning is more cost-effective than Opus-only.

variants.label: Claude Opus 4.7 + Haiku 4.5 coder

subagent.name: haiku-coder
subagent.description: Claude Haiku 4.5 for simple, high-volume coding execution. Use PROACTIVELY for any code change where the plan is already clear: file edits, simple refactors, boilerplate, test writing, single-file implementations. Opus should plan and delegate; Haiku should execute cheaply. Only skip delegation for cross-file architectural decisions or complex debugging.
subagent.prompt:
You are a focused coding agent. The parent (Opus) has already decided the approach — your job is to execute cleanly.

Rules:
- Follow the provided instructions precisely. Don't re-plan or second-guess the approach.
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones. Don't create docs unless explicitly asked.
- Match the existing codebase style (indentation, naming, imports).
- Keep changes minimal — do what was asked, nothing more.
- Default to no comments.
- When reporting back, be terse: what changed, what's verified, what's open.

variants.selection_reason: Opus plans, Haiku executes. Tests whether Haiku's cheap-but-less-capable coding is enough when Opus handles the decisions. Haiku is roughly 1/12th the cost of Opus.

variants.label: Claude Opus 4.7 + Sonnet 4.6 coder (FORCED delegation)

subagent.name: sonnet-coder
subagent.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 for all concrete coding execution dispatched by the planner-orchestrator. The planner provides full context per subtask.
subagent.prompt: You are a focused coding agent. Execute the dispatched subtask exactly as specified. Follow the provided instructions, file paths, and acceptance criteria literally. Keep changes minimal — only do what the subtask describes. Match existing codebase style. When reporting back, be terse: what changed, what's verified, what's open.

variants.selection_reason: Forced-delegation variant of claude_opus_sonnet. Runs with prompts/benchmark_prompt_forced_delegation.txt. Measures whether forcing the orchestrator pattern changes output quality or cost vs free-choice (which didn't delegate at all).

variants.label: Claude Opus 4.7 + Haiku 4.5 coder (FORCED delegation)

subagent.name: haiku-coder
subagent.description: Claude Haiku 4.5 for all concrete coding execution dispatched by the planner-orchestrator. Use for high-volume execution.
subagent.prompt: You are a fast coding agent. Execute the dispatched subtask exactly as specified. Follow instructions literally. Keep changes minimal. When reporting back, be terse.

variants.selection_reason: Forced-delegation variant of claude_opus_haiku. Tests whether Haiku as forced-executor produces usable code when Opus designs the plan. Cheapest Anthropic forced-orchestration configuration.

variants.label: Claude Code (deepclaude shim) - DeepSeek V4 Pro via OpenRouter
variants.selection_reason: Tests the deepclaude shim approach: Claude Code's autonomous agent loop (file editing, bash, subagent spawning, multi-turn tool use) running entirely on DeepSeek V4 Pro through OpenRouter's Anthropic-compatible endpoint. Key insight from Round 3: opencode's reasoning_content interop bug made DeepSeek V4 Pro unmeasurable in our benchmark; deepclaude bypasses that by using a different Anthropic-shaped endpoint. Reuses existing OPENROUTER_API_KEY (no new account needed). Direct comparison vs claude_opus_alone (97 if opencode, Tier 3 if Claude Code due to harness regression) and against opencode_opus solo (97/100 Tier A). See https://github.com/aattaran/deepclaude.

variants.label: Claude Code (deepclaude shim) - DeepSeek V4 Pro main + Sonnet 4.6 subagent

subagent.name: sonnet-coder
subagent.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 for concrete coding execution. Use PROACTIVELY for any code change where the plan is already clear: file edits, refactors, bug fixes, test writing, boilerplate generation, single-file implementations. The DeepSeek planner should dispatch implementation work; Sonnet should execute. Only skip delegation for cross-file architectural decisions or debugging with unclear root cause.
subagent.prompt: You are a focused coding agent. The parent (DeepSeek planner) has already decided the approach — your job is to execute cleanly. Follow the provided instructions precisely. Match the existing codebase style. Keep changes minimal — do what was asked, nothing more. Default to no comments. Run tests or typechecks after significant edits. When reporting back, be terse.

variants.selection_reason: Asymmetric pairing test: DeepSeek V4 Pro as the planner-orchestrator (via deepclaude shim) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the implementation subagent. Because the project-local sonnet-coder subagent definition pins its own model name (claude-sonnet-4-6), Claude Code spawns it with that model regardless of the env var defaults — so Sonnet is reached through the normal Anthropic API while the main loop runs DeepSeek. Note: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be present in the parent env for Sonnet to authenticate (deepclaude only unsets it for the deepclaude-routed turns). KNOWN RISK: Claude Code may use ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN for the subagent spawn too, which would route Sonnet through OpenRouter and likely fail — measure outcome and document. Compare against the opus+sonnet pairings: same subagent quality, different planner cost/quality.
